summary: The provided literature does not address cortisol levels, adrenal dysfunction, or salivary cortisol testing at all; thus, late-night salivary cortisol lacks basis as a superior screen compared to established standards in this patient group. No supporting evidence exists linking early morning serum cortisol measurements—standard practice—to outcomes specific to obesity and type 2 diabetes beyond general glucocorticoid metabolism.
